findings.title: Code injection via eval() on user input
findings.description: The eval() function is called directly on user_code parameter without any sanitization or validation. This allows arbitrary code execution including system commands, file operations, and malicious code that could compromise the entire system.
findings.fix: Replace eval() with a safe alternative like ast.literal_eval() for safe expression evaluation, or implement a proper sandboxed execution environment with restricted builtins and imports. Consider using libraries like RestrictedPython for safer code execution.
